021464.cherokee: ᎢᏣᏛᎬᎦ ᎠᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ; ᎦᏙ ᎢᏤᎵᎭ?  ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏕᎬᏭᎪᏓᏁᎴ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎬᏩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
021464.phonetic: I-tsa-dv-gv-ga a-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-gv-i; ga-do i-tse-li-ha?  ni-ga-dv-no de-gv-wu-go-da-ne-le ye-li-quo gv-wa-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i.

021465.english: And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.
021465.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎮ ᏕᎬᏩᎵᏥᏍᏆᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎧᏛ ᎬᏭᏢᏁᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏈᏅ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎮᎢ, ᎭᏙᎴᎰᎯ; ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᎮᎢ.
021465.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no u-na-le-nv-he de-gv-wa-li-tsi-s-qua-s-ge-i, a-le u-ka-dv gv-wu-tlv-ne-he-i, a-le a-s-qui-nv de-gv-wa-ni-di-s-ge-i, a-le hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-he-i, Ha-do-le-ho-hi; a-le ge-tsi-nv-si-da-s-di de-gv-wa-ni-he-i.

021466.english: And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:
021466.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎡᎳᏗᏢ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎡᏙᎮᎢ, ᎤᎷᏤ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᎯ ᎤᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ.
021466.phonetic: Qui-da-no e-la-di-tlv na-hna a-da-ne-lv e-do-he-i, u-lu-tse a-si-yv-wi a-ge-yv nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-hi u-nv-si-da-s-di.

021467.english: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.
021467.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎪᎲ ᏈᏓ ᎤᎦᎾᏬᏍᎬᎢ, ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᏂᎯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏥᏍᏕᏙᎲᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏗ ᎡᎯ.
021467.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-go-hv Qui-da u-ga-na-wo-s-gv-i, du-ka-hna-ne-i, a-le hi-a nu-we-se-i, Ni-hi na-s-quo tsi-s-de-do-hv-gi Tsi-sa Na-se-li-di e-hi.

021468.english: But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.
021468.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏓᏱᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᏱᎪᎵᎦ ᏂᏪᏍᎬᎢ.  ᎤᏄᎪᏤᏃ ᏙᏱᏗᏢ ᏩᏲᏓᏝᎲ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ; ᏣᏔᎦᏃ ᎤᏴᎵᏎᎢ.
021468.phonetic: A-se-no u-da-yi-le-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i, V-tla yi-tsi-ga-ta-ha, v-tla a-le yi-go-li-ga ni-we-s-gv-i.  U-nu-go-tse-no do-yi-di-tlv wa-yo-da-tla-hv wu-lo-se-i; tsa-ta-ga-no u-yv-li-se-i.

021469.english: And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.
021469.cherokee: ᎠᏛᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᎪᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎨᎳ.
021469.phonetic: A-dv-no ta-li-ne u-go-he-i, a-le u-le-nv-he hi-a ni-du-we-se-le na-v a-ni-do-na-i, hi-a na-s-gi na-hna ge-la.

021470.english: And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilaean, and thy speech agreeth thereto.
021470.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎤᏓᏱᎴᎢ.  ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎾᏃ ᎣᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎴ ᏈᏓ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᏂᎯ ᎾᎿᎭᎮᎳ; ᎨᎵᎵᏰᏃ ᎮᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎤᏠᏱ ᎢᎩ.
021470.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no u-da-yi-le-i.  Ni-go-hi-lv-na-no o-ni na-s-gi na-v a-ni-do-na-i ta-li-ne hi-a nu-ni-we-se-le Qui-da, U-do-hi-yu-hi ni-hi na-hna he-la; Ge-li-li-ye-no he-hi, a-le go-wo-ni-s-gv u-tlo-yi i-gi.

021471.english: But he began to curse and to swear, saying, I know not this man of whom ye speak.
021471.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᎤᏍᎩᏅᏕ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏎᎵᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏁᏪᏎᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᏥᎦᏔᎭ Ꮎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏤᏥᏃᎮᎭ.
021471.phonetic: A-se-no u-le-nv-he u-s-gi-nv-de a-le u-se-li-ta-ne hi-a ne-we-se-i, V-tla yi-tsi-ga-ta-ha na a-s-ga-ya na-s-gi tse-tsi-no-he-ha.

021472.english: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.
021472.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎤᏴᎵᏎ ᏣᏔᎦ.  ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏅᏓᏕ ᏥᏌ ᏄᏪᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᏏ ᏣᏔᎦ ᏔᎵ ᏄᏴᎳᏒᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏦᎢ ᏅᏓᏍᏆᏓᏱᎵ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸ ᏚᏠᏱᎴᎢ.
021472.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no u-yv-li-se tsa-ta-ga.  Qui-da-no u-nv-da-de Tsi-sa nu-we-sv-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-le-i, A-si tsa-ta-ga ta-li nu-yv-la-sv-na ge-se-s-di, tso-i nv-da-s-qua-da-yi-li.  Na-s-gi-no u-da-nv-te-lv du-tlo-yi-le-i.

021501.english: And straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him to Pilate.
021501.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᎩᏨᏅ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏛᏐᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᏂᎳᏫᎩ ᎤᎾᎵᏃᎮᎸ ᎤᎾᎸᎴ ᏥᏌ, ᎠᎴ  ᎤᎾᏘᎾᏫᏛᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏆᎴᏗ ᏫᏚᏂᏲᎯᏎᎴᎢ.
021501.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv wu-gi-tsv-nv nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi, a-le tsu-na-dv-so-nv-hi, a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi, a-le ni-ga-dv di-ni-la-wi-gi u-na-li-no-he-lv u-na-lv-le Tsi-sa, a-le  u-na-ti-na-wi-dv-he-i, a-le Qua-le-di wi-du-ni-yo-hi-se-le-i.

021502.english: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.
021502.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᎤᏛᏛᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏂᎯᏍᎪ ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ?  ᎤᏁᏨᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏰᎵᏉ ᏂᏫ.
021502.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no u-dv-dv-ne hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ni-hi-s-go Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-ga?  U-ne-tsv-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i, Ye-li-quo ni-wi.

021503.english: And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.

021504.english: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.
021504.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᏛᏛᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏝᏍᎪ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᏗᎭ?  ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᎭ ᎨᏣᏡᏗᎭ.
021504.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no ta-li-ne u-dv-dv-ne hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tla-s-go go-hu-s-di ya-di-ha?  Gv-ni-yu-quo u-tsa-ti tsu-da-le-nv-dv a-ni-no-he-ha ge-tsa-tlu-di-ha.

021505.english: But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.
021505.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏌ ᎠᏏ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᏗᏍᎨᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏆᎴᏗ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ.
021505.phonetic: A-se-no Tsi-sa a-si v-tla go-hu-s-di ya-di-s-ge-i; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di Qua-le-di u-s-qua-ni-go-se-i.

021506.english: Now at that feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.
021506.cherokee: ᏓᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᏃ ᎢᏳᎢ ᏓᏲᎯᏎᎮ ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᏴᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏉ ᎠᏂᏔᏲᎭ.
021506.phonetic: Da-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-no i-yu-i da-yo-hi-se-he a-si-yv-wi a-yv-gi, na-s-gi-quo a-ni-ta-yo-ha.

021507.english: And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.
021507.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏴᏫᏃ ᎡᎮ ᏆᎳᏆ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏕᎨᎦᎸᎡ ᎬᏩᎵᎪᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏖᎸᏅᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᎸᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏖᎸᏅᎢ.
021507.phonetic: A-si-yv-wi-no e-he Qua-la-qua tsu-do-i-dv na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha de-ge-ga-lv-e gv-wa-li-go-ne-lv-hi u-na-da-su-ye-dv u-na-li-te-lv-nv-hi, na-s-gi u-na-da-lv-hi ge-se na-hi-yu u-na-da-su-ye-dv u-na-li-te-lv-nv-i.

021508.english: And the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them.
021508.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏕᎢ, ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎮ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᎴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏚᏛᏁᎸ ᏧᏩᎫᏔᏅᏒ ᎢᏧᏛᏁᏗᏱ.
021508.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no a-s-da-ya u-na-li-s-de-i, u-na-le-nv-he u-ni-ta-yo-le na-s-gi-ya ni-du-dv-ne-lv tsu-wa-gu-ta-nv-sv i-tsu-dv-ne-di-yi.

021509.english: But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
021509.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᏚᏁᏤᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎢᏣᏚᎵᏍᎪ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᏗᏳᏲᎯᏎᏗᏱ.
021509.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no du-ne-tse-le hi-a nu-we-se-i, I-tsa-du-li-s-go U-gv-wi-yu-hi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-ga di-yu-yo-hi-se-di-yi.

021510.english: For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.
021510.cherokee: ᎠᎦᏔᎮᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᏅᏳᏤᎲᏉ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏕᎬᏩᏲᏒᎢ.
021510.phonetic: A-ga-ta-he-ye-no na-s-gi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi a-nv-yu-tse-hv-quo nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv de-gv-wa-yo-sv-i.

021511.english: But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.
021511.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏄᏂᎬᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᏄᏅᏂᏌᏁ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏆᎳᏆ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏲᎯᏎᏗᏱ.
021511.phonetic: A-se-no nu-ni-gv-yu-sv a-tsi-lv--a-ne-lo-hi nu-nv-ni-sa-ne u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi, na-s-gi Qua-la-qua u-ni-ta-yo-s-di-yi tsu-yo-hi-se-di-yi.

021512.english: And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do unto him whom ye call the King of the Jews?
021512.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᏁᏨ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᏣᏚᎵ ᏥᏴᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᏤᏦᏎᎭ?
021512.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no ta-li-ne u-ne-tsv, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no i-tsa-du-li tsi-yv-ne-di-yi na-s-gi na U-gv-wi-yu-hi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-ga tse-tso-se-ha?

021513.english: And they cried out again, Crucify him.

021514.english: Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.
021514.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᏙᏃ, ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎤᏲ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ?  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏟᏉ ᎢᏯᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏁᎷᏁᎢ, ᎯᏯᏛᎥᎦ, ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎢ.
021514.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, Ga-do-no, ga-do u-s-di u-yo du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv?  A-se-no u-tli-quo i-ya-s-da-ya u-ne-lu-ne-i, hi-ya-dv-v-ga, u-na-dv-ne-i.

021515.english: And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.
021515.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏎ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ, ᏚᏲᎯᏎᎴ ᏆᎳᏆ, ᏥᏌᏃ ᎤᎵᎥᏂᎸ ᏚᏲᏎ ᎠᎦᏛᏗᏱ.
021515.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no o-si-yu u-ye-lv-se o-s-dv tsu-da-nv-da-di-s-do-di-yi u-ni-tsa-ti, du-yo-hi-se-le Qua-la-qua, Tsi-sa-no u-li-v-ni-lv du-yo-se a-ga-dv-di-yi.

021516.english: And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Praetorium; and they call together the whole band.
021516.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩᏃ ᎬᏩᏘᎾᏫᏛᎮ ᏫᎬᏩᏴᏔᏁ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏱ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏣᏥᎳᏫᏤᏗᏱ, ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᏫᏚᏂᏯᏅᎮᎢ.
021516.phonetic: A-ni-ya-wi-s-gi-no gv-wa-ti-na-wi-dv-he wi-gv-wa-yv-ta-ne u-gv-wi-yu-hi-yi a-da-ne-lv-i na-s-gi u-gv-wi-yu-hi tsa-tsi-la-wi-tse-di-yi, ni-ga-dv-no su-na-da-tlu-gi wi-du-ni-ya-nv-he-i.

021517.english: And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head,
021517.cherokee: ᎩᎦᎨᏃ ᎬᏩᏄᏪᎡᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎵᏍᏚᎶ ᏧᏣᏲᏍᏗ ᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏍᏕᏲᏅ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏍᏚᎶᏔᏁᎢ.
021517.phonetic: Gi-ga-ge-no gv-wa-nu-we-e-i, a-le a-li-s-du-lo tsu-tsa-yo-s-di go-tlv-ta-nv-hi u-ni-s-de-yo-nv, na-s-gi u-ni-s-du-lo-ta-ne-i.

021518.english: And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!
021518.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᎴᏅᎮ ᎠᏂᏲᎵᎮᎢ, ᎢᏨᏲᎵᎦ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎾᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
021518.phonetic: A-le u-na-le-nv-he a-ni-yo-li-he-i, i-tsv-yo-li-ga, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi A-ni-tsu-si u-na-tse-li-ga, a-na-di-s-ge-i.

021519.english: And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him.
021519.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎪᎵᏃ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᎮ ᎦᎾᏍᏓ ᎬᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎬᏩᎵᏥᏍᏆᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎾᎵᏂᏆᏅᎥᏍᎨ ᎬᏩᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎮᎢ.
021519.phonetic: A-s-go-li-no de-gv-wa-ni-he ga-na-s-da gv-di, a-le de-gv-wa-li-tsi-s-qua-s-ge-i, a-le da-na-li-ni-qua-nv-v-s-ge gv-wa-da-do-li-s-da-ne-he-i.

021520.english: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.
021520.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏕᎰᏔᏃᏅᏃ, ᎬᏩᏄᏪᏎ ᎩᎦᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏧᏄᏬ ᏕᎬᏩᏄᏬᎡᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᎬᏩᏘᏅᏍᏔᏁ ᎬᏩᏛᏗᏱ.
021520.phonetic: U-na-de-ho-ta-no-nv-no, gv-wa-nu-we-se gi-ga-ge-i, a-le u-wa-sv tsu-nu-wo de-gv-wa-nu-wo-e-i, a-le wi-gv-wa-ti-nv-s-ta-ne gv-wa-dv-di-yi.

021521.english: And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.
021521.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏌᎵᏂ ᎡᎯ ᎠᎢᏎᎢ, ᏙᏗᎦᎶᎨᏒ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎵᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎷᏆᏏ ᎤᏂᏙᏓ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏧᏓᎿᎭᏩᏛ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏄᏅᏁᎴᎢ.
021521.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di Sa-wa-ni tsu-do-i-dv Sa-li-ni e-hi a-i-se-i, do-di-ga-lo-ge-sv nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi, na-s-gi E-li-gi a-le Lu-qua-si u-ni-do-da, na-s-gi u-tse-li tsu-da-hna-wa-dv u-we-nv-s-di-yi nu-nv-ne-le-i.

021522.english: And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.
021522.cherokee: ᎪᎵᎦᏓᏃ ᏚᏙᎥ ᏫᎬᏩᏘᏅᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏍᏆᎷᎪ ᎦᏛᎦ.
021522.phonetic: Go-li-ga-da-no du-do-v wi-gv-wa-ti-nv-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi a-ne-tlv-ta-nv-hi ge-sv u-s-qua-lu-go ga-dv-ga.

021523.english: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not.
021523.cherokee: ᎬᏩᏁᏁᎴᏃ ᎤᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎩᎦᎨᎠᏗᏔᏍᏗ ᎻᎳ ᎠᏑᎵ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᏁᎩᏎᎢ.
021523.phonetic: Gv-wa-ne-ne-le-no u-di-ta-s-di gi-ga-ge-a-di-ta-s-di mi-la a-su-li; a-se-no v-tla yu-ne-gi-se-i.

021524.english: And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.
021524.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏓᏓᎿᎭᏩᏍᏛ ᎬᏩᏛᏅ ᎤᏂᏯᏙᎴ ᏧᏄᏬᎢ, ᎤᎾᏎᏒᎮ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏏᏴᏫᎭ ᏧᏂᏁᏒᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
021524.phonetic: Hna-quo-no da-da-hna-wa-s-dv gv-wa-dv-nv u-ni-ya-do-le tsu-nu-wo-i, u-na-se-sv-he u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi na-s-gi a-ni-si-yv-wi-ha tsu-ni-ne-sv-s-di ge-sv-i.

021525.english: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.
021525.cherokee: ᏐᎣᏁᎳᏃ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎧᎳᏩᏗᏒ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏛᏁᎢ.
021525.phonetic: So-o-ne-la-no i-yu-tli-lo-dv ka-la-wa-di-sv ge-se-i, a-le gv-wa-dv-ne-i.

021526.english: And the superscription of his accusation was written over, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
021526.cherokee: ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏢᏃ ᎪᏪᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎫᎢᏍᏛᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏁᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
021526.phonetic: Ga-lv-la-di-tlv-no go-we-le nu-s-dv a-gu-i-s-dv-i, hi-a ni-gv-ne-i, U-Gv-Wi-Yu-Hi A-Ni-Tsu-Si U-Na-Tse-Li-Ga.

021527.english: And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.
021527.cherokee: ᎢᏧᎳᎭᏃ ᏕᎨᎦᏛᏁ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᏗᎾᏓᎾᏌᎲᏍᎩ; ᎠᏏᏴᏫ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ, ᏐᎢᏃ ᎠᎦᏍᎦᏂ ᎢᏗᏢ.
021527.phonetic: I-tsu-la-ha-no de-ge-ga-dv-ne a-ni-ta-li di-na-da-na-sa-hv-s-gi; a-si-yv-wi a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv, so-i-no a-ga-s-ga-ni i-di-tlv.

021528.english: And the scripture was fulfilled, which saith, And he was numbered with the transgressors.
021528.cherokee: ᎪᏪᎸᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎦᏪᎭ, ᎤᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᎨᎦᏎᎸ ᎠᎦᏠᏯᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
021528.phonetic: Go-we-lv-no u-do-hi-yu-ne-i, hi-a tsi-ni-ga-we-ha, u-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi ge-ga-se-lv a-ga-tlo-ya-s-ta-nv-gi.

021529.english: And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days,
021529.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎶᏍᎩᏃ ᎦᎬᏩᏐᏢᏗᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᎾᎵᏍᏛᏂᎮ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ, Ᏺ, ᎤᏛᏅ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎯᏲᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢᏉᏉ ᎢᎦ ᎯᏱᎵᏙᎯ ᎭᏁᏍᎨᏍᎩ,
021529.phonetic: A-ni-lo-s-gi-no ga-gv-wa-so-tlv-di-s-ge-i, a-le da-na-li-s-dv-ni-he hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i, Yo, u-dv-nv--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi hi-yo-s-di-s-gi, a-le tso-i-quo-quo i-ga hi-yi-li-do-hi ha-ne-s-ge-s-gi,

021530.english: Save thyself, and come down from the cross.

021531.english: Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.
021531.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ ᎠᏁᎶᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏕᎰᏗᏍᎬ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏓᎾᏓᏪᏎᎮᎢ, ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᏓᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬᎩ; ᎤᏩᏒ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎬᎵᏍᏕᎸ.
021531.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-lv a-ne-lo-hi a-le di-no-we-li-s-gi gv-wa-de-ho-di-s-gv, hi-a ni-da-na-da-we-se-he-i, Nv-wa-na-da-le da-s-de-li-s-gv-gi; u-wa-sv v-tla yv-gv-li-s-de-lv.

021532.english: Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.
021532.cherokee: ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏩᏠᎠᎯ ᏓᏓᎿᎭᏩᏍᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᎩᎪᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎣᎪᎯᏳᏗᏱ.  ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏗᎨᎦᏛᏅᎯ ᎦᎬᏩᏐᏢᏕᎢ.
021532.phonetic: Ga-lo-ne-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi I-si-li u-na-tse-li-ga hna-quo wa-tlo-a-hi da-da-hna-wa-s-dv-i, na-s-gi o-gi-go-wa-dv-di-yi a-le o-go-hi-yu-di-yi.  A-le na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha di-ge-ga-dv-nv-hi ga-gv-wa-so-tlv-de-i.

021533.english: And when the sixth hour was come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
021533.cherokee: ᏔᎳᏚᏃ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎤᎵᏰᎢᎶᎸ ᎤᎵᏏᎲᏎ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏙᎯ, ᏦᎢ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎢᏯᏍᏘ.
021533.phonetic: Ta-la-du-no i-yu-tli-lo-dv u-li-ye-i-lo-lv u-li-si-hv-se ni-gv-na-dv na-hna ga-do-hi, tso-i i-yu-tli-lo-dv i-ya-s-ti.

021534.english: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
021534.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏁ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎤᎵᏰᎢᎶᎸ ᏥᏌ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᏪᎷᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎢᎶᎢ, ᎢᎶᎢ, ᎳᎹ ᏌᏆᏓᏂ?  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎦᏛᎦ, ᎠᏆᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᏆᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎥᏍᏋᏕᎩ?
021534.phonetic: Tso-i-ne i-yu-tli-lo-dv u-li-ye-i-lo-lv Tsi-sa a-s-da-ya u-we-lu-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i, I-lo-i, I-lo-i, la-ma sa-qua-da-ni?  Na-s-gi a-ne-tlv-ta-nv-hi hi-a nu-s-di ga-dv-ga, A-qua-ne-la-nv-hi, A-qua-ne-la-nv-hi, ga-do-no v-s-quv-de-gi?

021535.english: And some of them that stood by, when they heard it, said, Behold, he calleth Elias.
021535.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎾᎥ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎢ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᎢᎳᏯ ᎠᏯᏂᎭ.
021535.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no na-v a-ni-do-na-i u-na-dv-ga-nv, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, Gv-ni-yu-quo, I-la-ya a-ya-ni-ha.

021536.english: And one ran and filled a spunge full of vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone; let us see whether Elias will come to take him down.
021536.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏴᏫᏃ ᏚᏍᏆᎸᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏬᎵ ᏧᏂᏦᏯᏍᏗ ᏭᎧᎵᎢᏍᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎾᏍᏙᎯ ᎤᏪᏆᏔᏅ, ᎤᏁᏁᎸ ᎤᏗᏔᏍᏗ; ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᏁᎳᎩ, ᎢᏓᏙᎴᎰᎯ, ᏥᎪᏃ ᎢᎳᏯ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ ᏓᏳᏠᎥᏔᏂᎵ.
021536.phonetic: A-se-yv-wi-no du-s-qua-lv-ta-nv, a-le du-wo-li tsu-ni-tso-ya-s-di wu-ka-li-i-s-ta-nv, a-le ga-na-s-do-hi u-we-qua-ta-nv, u-ne-ne-lv u-di-ta-s-di; hi-a nu-we-se-i, U-ne-la-gi, i-da-do-le-ho-hi, tsi-go-no I-la-ya da-ga-lu-tsi da-yu-tlo-v-ta-ni-li.

021537.english: And Jesus cried with a loud voice, and gave up the ghost.

021538.english: And the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom.
021538.cherokee: ᎠᏰᏙᎳᏛᏗᏃ ᎤᏛᏅ-ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏛ ᎤᏓᏣᎦᎸᎮ ᏔᎵ ᏄᏓᏕᎢ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏩᏍᏗ.
021538.phonetic: A-ye-do-la-dv-di-no u-dv-nv--di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi ga-dv u-da-tsa-ga-lv-he ta-li nu-da-de-i, ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-dv e-la-di wa-s-di.

021539.english: And when the centurion, which stood over against him, saw that he so cried out, and gave up the ghost, he said, Truly this man was the Son of God.
021539.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈᏃ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎢᏗᏢ ᏧᏳᎪᏗ ᎦᏙᎩ, ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏕᎸᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏥ ᎨᏎᎢ.
021539.phonetic: A-s-go-hi-tsu-qui-no di-ti-ni-do-hi a-i-di-tlv tsu-yu-go-di ga-do-gi, u-do-le-ho-sv na-s-gi u-we-lu-nv-i, a-le u-li-s-qua-de-lv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i, U-do-hi-yu-hi hi-a a-s-ga-ya U-ne-la-nv-hi U-we-tsi ge-se-i.

021540.english: There were also women looking on afar off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;
021540.cherokee: ᎠᏂᎨᏴᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ Ꭸ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏙᏗᏂᎧᏁᎢ; ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎠᏁᎴ ᎺᎵ ᏑᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎺᎵ ᏥᎻ ᎤᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏦᏏ ᎤᏂᏥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᎶᎻ.
021540.phonetic: A-ni-ge-yv-no na-s-quo ge i-yv-dv do-di-ni-ka-ne-i; na-hna-no a-ne-le Me-li Su-gi-de-li e-hi, a-le Me-li Tsi-mi u-s-di a-le Tso-si u-ni-tsi-i, a-le Sa-lo-mi.

021541.english: (Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.
021541.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎨᎵᎵ ᏤᏙᎮ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎮ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎮᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏙᎸᎯ.
021541.phonetic: Na-s-gi na-s-quo Ge-li-li tse-do-he gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-he a-le gv-wa-s-de-lv-hi-do-he-i; a-le u-ni-tsa-te nv-wa-na-da-le a-ni-ge-yv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi nv-da-gv-wa-s-da-wa-do-lv-hi.

021542.english: And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
021542.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏒ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏛᏅᎢᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏈᏕᎾ ᎢᎦ ᎨᏒᎢ.
021542.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-sv nu-li-s-ta-nv, na-s-gi a-dv-nv-i-s-do-di-yi ge-sv i-yu-s-di, na-s-gi u-na-do-da-qui-de-na i-ga ge-sv-i.

021543.english: Joseph of Arimathaea, and honourable counseller, which also waited for the kingdom of God, came, and went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus.
021543.cherokee: ᏦᏩ ᎠᎵᎹᏗᏱ ᎡᎯ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᎦᏘᏰ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎷᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᏭᏴᎴ ᏆᎴᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏔᏲᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ.
021543.phonetic: Tso-wa A-li-ma-di-yi e-hi a-tsi-lv-quo-di di-ga-la-wi-gi, na-s-gi na-s-quo a-ga-ti-ye U-ne-la-nv-hi u-gv-wi-yu-hi ge-sv u-lu-hi-s-di-yi, na-s-gi u-lu-tse-i, a-le na-s-ga-i-hv-na wu-yv-le Qua-le-di-yi, a-le u-ta-yo-le Tsi-sa a-ye-lv-i.

021544.english: And Pilate marvelled if he were already dead: and calling unto him the centurion, he asked him whether he had been any while dead.
021544.cherokee: ᏆᎴᏗᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᏏᎪᏎ ᎤᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᎮ ᎾᏞᎬ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ; ᏭᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᎤᏛᏛᏁ, ᏰᎵᏍᎪ ᎪᎯᎩ ᎬᏩᏲᎱᏒᎯ, ᎤᏛᏁᎢ.
021544.phonetic: Qua-le-di-no u-s-qua-si-go-se u-dla-si-dv-e-he na-tle-gv u-yo-hu-sv-i; wu-ya-nv-hv-no a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi, u-dv-dv-ne, ye-li-s-go go-hi-gi gv-wa-yo-hu-sv-hi, u-dv-ne-i.

021545.english: And when he knew it of the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
021545.cherokee: ᎬᏂᎨᏒᏃ ᏄᏩᏁᎸ ᎠᏍᎪᎯᏧᏈ ᏗᏘᏂᏙᎯ, ᏦᏩ ᏚᏲᎯᏎᎴ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ.
021545.phonetic: Gv-ni-ge-sv-no nu-wa-ne-lv a-s-go-hi-tsu-qui di-ti-ni-do-hi, Tso-wa du-yo-hi-se-le a-ye-lv-i.

021546.english: And he bought fine linen, and took him down, and wrapped him in the linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock, and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre.
021546.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏒᏃ ᎤᏏᏙᎵ ᏙᎴᏛ ᎠᏄᏬ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᏒ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏄᏬᎩᎯ ᏙᎴᏛ ᎤᏪᏣᏄᎳᏅ, ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᏅᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏲᎯ ᎠᏍᎪᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏯ ᎤᏪᏌᏆᎴᎴᎢ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎢ.
021546.phonetic: U-wa-sv-no u-si-do-li do-le-dv a-nu-wo, a-le u-de-sv a-ye-lv-i, a-le a-nu-wo-gi-hi do-le-dv u-we-tsa-nu-la-nv, a-tse-li-s-dv u-nv-ne na-s-gi nv-yo-hi a-s-go-sv-i, a-le nv-ya u-we-sa-qua-le-le-i a-tse-li-s-dv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi u-ne-i.

021547.english: And Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses beheld where he was laid.
021547.cherokee: ᎺᎵᏃ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎺᎵ ᏦᏏ ᎤᏥ ᎤᏂᎪᎮ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏂᏅᏅᎢ.
021547.phonetic: Me-li-no Ma-gi-de-li e-hi, a-le Me-li Tso-si u-tsi u-ni-go-he na-hna u-ni-nv-nv-i.

021601.english: And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.
021601.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᏃ ᎤᎶᏐᏅ, ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎺᎵ ᏥᎻ ᎤᏥ ᎠᎴ ᏌᎶᎻ ᎤᏂᏩᏒᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎤᎦᎾᏍᏛ ᎦᏩᏒᎩ, ᎤᏂᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏎ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᎶᏁᏗᏱ.
021601.phonetic: U-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-no u-lo-so-nv, Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi, a-le Me-li Tsi-mi u-tsi a-le Sa-lo-mi u-ni-wa-sv-hi ge-se u-ga-na-s-dv ga-wa-sv-gi, u-ni-lu-hi-s-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-se a-le gv-wa-lo-ne-di-yi.

021602.english: And very early in the morning the first day of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.
021602.cherokee: ᏑᎾᎴᎢᏳᏃ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎢᎦ ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᏨᏗᎧᎸᎪᏉ.
021602.phonetic: Su-na-le-i-yu-no i-gv-yi-yi i-ga su-na-do-da-qua-s-di ge-sv-i, u-ni-lu-tse a-tse-li-s-dv tsv-di-ka-lv-go-quo.

021603.english: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?
021603.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᎾᏓᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎦᎪ ᏓᎩᎲᎡᎵ ᏅᏯ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎲᎢ?
021603.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-na-da-we-se-le-i, Ga-go da-gi-hv-e-li nv-ya a-tse-li-s-dv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi a-hv-i?

021604.english: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.
021604.cherokee: ᏫᏚᎾᎧᎿᎭᏅᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎮ ᏅᏯ ᎠᎲᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᏣᏘᏰᏃ ᎡᏉᎯᏳ ᎨᏎᎢ.
021604.phonetic: Wi-du-na-ka-hna-nv-no u-ni-go-he nv-ya a-hv-dv ge-sv-i, u-tsa-ti-ye-no e-quo-hi-yu ge-se-i.

021605.english: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.
021605.cherokee: ᏭᏂᏴᎸᏃ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ, ᎤᏂᎪᎮ ᎠᏫᏴ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ ᎤᏬᎴᎢ, ᎤᏄᏪ ᎦᏅᎯᏛ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎠᏄᏬ; ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮᏃ.
021605.phonetic: Wu-ni-yv-lv-no a-tse-li-s-dv-i, u-ni-go-he a-wi-yv a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv u-wo-le-i, u-nu-we ga-nv-hi-dv u-ne-gv a-nu-wo; a-ni-s-ga-i-he-no.

021606.english: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.
021606.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏥᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ; ᏥᏌ ᎾᏎᎵᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎡᏥᏲᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᎦᏛᎥᏍᎬᎩ; ᏚᎴᎯᏌᏅ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᏂ ᏱᎦᏅ; ᏗᏣᎧᏅᎦ Ꭰ  ᎤᏂᏅᏅᎢ.
021606.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, Tle-s-di yi-tsi-s-ga-i-he-s-di; Tsi-sa Na-se-li-di e-hi e-tsi-yo-ha, na-s-gi tsa-ga-dv-v-s-gv-gi; du-le-hi-sa-nv; v-tla a-ni yi-ga-nv; di-tsa-ka-nv-ga a  u-ni-nv-nv-i.

021607.english: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
021607.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏥᏤᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏗᏥᏃᎲᏏ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏈᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏫᏚᎶᎯᏎᏗᏱ ᎨᎵᎵ; ᎾᎿᎭᏓᏰᏥᎪᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏥᏪᏎᎸᎢ.
021607.phonetic: A-se-no tsi-tse-na, a-le wi-di-tsi-no-hv-si gv-wa-s-da-wa-di-do-hi a-le Qui-da na-s-gi i-gv-yi wi-du-lo-hi-se-di-yi Ge-li-li; na-hna da-ye-tsi-go-i, na-s-gi-ya ni-tsi-we-se-lv-i.

021608.english: And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any man; for they were afraid.
021608.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏍᏗᏃ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨ ᏚᎾᎵᏖᏎ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛᎢ; ᏚᏂᎾᏫᏍᎨᏰᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏳᏃᏎᎴᎢ; ᎠᏂᏍᎦᎢᎮᏰᏃ.
021608.phonetic: U-li-s-di-no u-ni-nu-go-tsv du-na-li-te-se a-tse-li-s-dv-i; du-ni-na-wi-s-ge-ye-no a-le u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-s-ge-i; v-tla a-le gi-lo go-hu-s-di yu-no-se-le-i; a-ni-s-ga-i-he-ye-no.

021609.english: Now when Jesus was risen early the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had cast seven devils.
021609.cherokee: ᏥᏌᏃ ᏚᎴᎯᏌᏅ ᏑᎾᎴᎢᏳ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎢᎦ ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᎬᏱ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏛᏁᎴ ᎺᎵ ᎹᎩᏕᎵ ᎡᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏥᏚᏄᎪᏫᏎᎴᎢ.
021609.phonetic: Tsi-sa-no du-le-hi-sa-nv su-na-le-i-yu i-gv-yi-yi i-ga su-na-do-da-qua-s-di ge-sv-i, i-gv-yi gv-ni-ge-sv nu-dv-ne-le Me-li Ma-gi-de-li e-hi, na-s-gi ga-li-quo-gi a-ni-s-gi-na tsi-du-nu-go-wi-se-le-i.

021610.english: And she went and told them that had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
021610.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏪᏅ ᎤᏁᏙᎸᎯ ᏫᏚᏃᏁᎴ ᎤᏲ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎾᏠᏱᎲᎢ.
021610.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-we-nv u-ne-do-lv-hi wi-du-no-ne-le u-yo du-na-da-nv-dv-i a-le da-na-tlo-yi-hv-i.

021611.english: And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.
021611.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎬᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎪᎲᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏳᏃᎯᏳᏁᎢ.
021611.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv gv-nv-i, a-le na-s-gi u-go-hv-i, v-tla yu-no-hi-yu-ne-i.

021612.english: After that he appeared in another form unto two of them, as they walked, and went into the country.
021612.cherokee: ᎣᏂᏃ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᏄᏍᏕ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏛᏁᎴ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎳ ᎠᎾᎢᏎᎢ, ᏙᏗᎦᎶᎨᏒ ᏩᏂᎦᏖᎢ.
021612.phonetic: O-ni-no nv-wa-da-le nu-s-de gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-dv-ne-le a-ni-ta-li na-hna a-ne-la a-na-i-se-i, do-di-ga-lo-ge-sv wa-ni-ga-te-i.

021613.english: And they went and told it unto the residue: neither believed they them.
021613.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏁᏅ ᏫᏚᏂᏃᏁᎴ ᎠᏂᏐᎢ; ᎾᏍᏉ ᎥᏝ ᏱᏚᏃᎯᏳᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ.
021613.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ne-nv wi-du-ni-no-ne-le a-ni-so-i; na-s-quo v-tla yi-du-no-hi-yu-ne na-s-gi.

021614.english: Afterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat, and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen.
021614.cherokee: ᎣᏂᏃ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏛᏁᎴ ᏌᏚ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎬᏍᎪᎸᏁ ᏂᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏍᏓᏱᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ, ᏂᏚᏃᎯᏳᏅᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎪᎲᎯ ᏧᎴᎯᏌᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏎᎢ.
021614.phonetic: O-ni-no gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-dv-ne-le sa-du i-ya-ni-dv a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-i, a-le du-gv-s-go-lv-ne ni-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge nu-no-hi-yu-sv-na ge-sv a-le di-s-da-yi-yu ge-sv tsu-ni-na-wi, ni-du-no-hi-yu-nv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di na-s-gi gv-wa-go-hv-hi tsu-le-hi-sa-nv-hi ni-ge-se-i.

021615.english: And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.
021615.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎢᏤᏙᎾ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏂᎬᎾᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏗᏣᎵᏥᏙᎲᏏ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎨᎪᏢᏅᎯ.
021615.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-le-i, I-tse-do-na e-lo-hi ni-gv-na-dv-i, a-le wi-di-tsa-li-tsi-do-hv-si o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv na-ni-v ge-go-tlv-nv-hi.

021616.english: He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.
021616.cherokee: ᎩᎶ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏬᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ; ᏂᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᏣᎫᎪᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
021616.phonetic: Gi-lo go-hi-yu-hv-s-ge-s-di a-le a-ga-wo-s-ge-s-di a-tsi-s-de-lv-di ge-se-s-di; ni-go-hi-yu-hv-s-gv-na-s-gi-ni tsa-gu-go-da-ne-di ge-se-s-di.

021617.english: And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;
021617.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᏗᎪᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏔᎵ ᏚᏛᏗᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ; ᎠᏴ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᎠᏅᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏓᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᏧᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᏓᏂᏬᏂᏍᎨᏍᏗ;
021617.phonetic: Hi-a-no na-s-gi u-ye-lv-dv di-go-li-s-do-di ta-li du-dv-di-de-ge-s-di a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi; a-yv da-qua-do-v a-nv-di-s-ge-s-di a-ni-s-gi-na da-ni-nu-go-wi-s-ge-s-di; nv-wa-na-da-le tsu-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di da-ni-wo-ni-s-ge-s-di;

021618.english: They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.
021618.cherokee: ᎢᎾᏛ ᏓᏂᏁᏍᎨᏍᏗ; ᎢᏳ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎠᏓᎯᎯ ᏳᎾᏗᏔᎲ, ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏳᏅᏁᎮᏍᏗ; ᏧᏂᏢᎩ ᏓᎾᏏᏔᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏗᏩᏍᎨᏍᏗ.
021618.phonetic: I-na-dv da-ni-ne-s-ge-s-di; i-yu a-le go-hu-s-di a-da-hi-hi yu-na-di-ta-hv, v-tla go-hu-s-di yu-nv-ne-he-s-di; tsu-ni-tlv-gi da-na-si-ta-di-s-ge-s-di, a-le du-na-di-wa-s-ge-s-di.

021619.english: So then after the Lord had spoken unto them, he was received up into heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.
021619.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅ, ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏙᏣᏕᏓᏂᎸᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏘᏏ ᎢᏗᏢ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏬᎸ ᎤᏪᏁᎢ.
021619.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi du-wo-ne-ta-nv, ga-lv-la-di do-tsa-de-da-ni-lv-tse-i, a-le a-ga-ti-si i-di-tlv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-wo-lv u-we-ne-i.

021620.english: And they went forth, and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word with signs following. Amen.
021620.cherokee: ᎤᏁᏅᏎᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎤᎾᎵᏥᏙᏂᏙᎴᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏔᎵ ᎤᏛᏗᏕᎨ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏁᎬ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎤᎵᏠᏯᏍᏗᏕᎬᎢ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
021620.phonetic: U-ne-nv-se-no a-le ni-gv-na-dv u-na-li-tsi-do-ni-do-le-i, U-gv-wi-yu-hi ta-li u-dv-di-de-ge du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, a-le a-ni-ne-gv a-s-da-yi-di-s-di-s-ge u-ye-lv-dv u-li-tlo-ya-s-di-de-gv-i.  E-me-nv.
